name: Dock Ellis
facts: Famously pitched a no-hitter in 1970 while under the influence of LSD.
Was a vocal advocate for players' rights and African American athletes.
Once hit three consecutive batters to 'straighten them out'.

name: Bartolo Colón
facts: Known affectionately as 'Big Sexy' by fans and teammates.
Became the oldest player to hit his first career home run at age 42.
Played for 11 different MLB franchises over 21 seasons.

name: Munenori Kawasaki
facts: Famous for his high-energy post-game interviews and 'I am Japanese!' catchphrase.
Authored a 'handbook' on how to be a teammate.
Once told reporters his secret to avoiding cramps was eating three bananas a day.

name: Willie Bloomquist
facts: Played for and coached the Arizona State Sundevils baseball team.
The ultimate utility man. He played multiple positions throughout his career.
Kindof looks like Derek Lowe and Justin Verlander had a baby.

name: Jason Bay
teams: Padres, Pirates, Red Sox, Mets, Mariners
facts: The first Pirate to win the National League Rookie of the Year award since Barry Bonds in 1986.
About equally as attractive as Pau Skenes.
